pipeline.stopRequested: Stop requested. Glossa will finish the current chunk and then stop.
pipeline.stopConfirmed: Pipeline stopped after the current chunk.
pipeline.candidatePlaceholder: Output will appear here. Edit manually before auditing...
pipeline.chunkPreviewHint: Review source chunks before running the pipeline. You can edit, split, or merge chunks while the pipeline is idle.
pipeline.confirmClearMessage: All chunks, candidate drafts, and audit results will be discarded. This cannot be undone.
pipeline.confirmRemoveStageMessage: Stage "{{name}}" will be deleted from the pipeline. You can disable it instead to keep its configuration.
pipeline.unlockSourceHint: Editing the source will discard this chunk's translation, stage outputs, and audit.
pipeline.confirmUnlockMessage: The translation, all stage outputs, and the audit for this chunk will be discarded so you can edit. This cannot be undone.
pipeline.rerunAllHint: Discard the {{count}} completed translation(s) and run the pipeline from scratch.
pipeline.confirmRerunAllMessage: {{count}} completed translation(s) and their audits will be discarded before the pipeline restarts. This cannot be undone.
pipeline.auditSkippedNoDraft: Nothing to audit yet — translate this chunk first.
pipeline.glossaryEmpty: No terms in the registry. Use + to add one.

audit.title: Audit Logs
audit.compositeTooltip: Semantic quality synthesized across all completed chunks.

settings.title: Global Config / Secrets
settings.securityMessage: API keys are stored in your operating system's keychain when available. If the keychain is unavailable, Glossa falls back to a local encrypted file protected by a machine-local key; this is less secure than the OS keychain. Keys never leave your device. Ollama runs entirely on your machine — no data is sent to external servers.
settings.keySavedFallback: {{provider}} key saved to local encrypted fallback storage. This is less secure than the OS keychain.

ollama.title: Ollama (Local Models)
ollama.notRunning: Ollama is not running. Start it with 'ollama serve' to use local models.
ollama.noModels: Ollama is running but no models are installed. Run 'ollama pull <model>' to get started.
ollama.selectedButOffline: You picked Ollama but it isn't connected — check Settings
ollama.uncheckedHint: Status unchecked. Click "Refresh" to verify the connection and load local models.

projects.title: Projects
projects.firstSaveTitle: Save this draft as a project
projects.firstSaveDescription: You are working in an unsaved session. Give the project a name to persist text, configuration, glossary, and results.
projects.empty: No projects yet. Create one to save your work.
projects.confirmDeleteMessage: "{{name}}" will be permanently removed, including all saved translations. This cannot be undone.

files.importPreviewTitle: Review the document before creating chunks
files.importPreviewHint: Adjust segmentation before the document replaces the current workspace.
files.importExperimentalDocxMarkdown: DOCX import converted to Markdown. Check emphasis, footnotes, and block boundaries before translating.

document.manualSplitTitle: Choose where this chunk should break
document.manualSplitHint: Place the text cursor in the source chunk. The preview updates before you confirm.
document.noStageTrace: No output available for this stage.
document.translationLockedBody: This translation is protected from accidental edits. You can unlock it from the green check button in the chunk toolbar.
document.configDrawerHint: Edit stages, glossary and providers without covering the document text.
document.insightsDrawerHint: Chunk index and judge findings for the current chunk.
document.insightsAuditEmpty: No judge result for this chunk yet.
document.insightsAuditNoIssues: No issues raised by the judge.
document.sourceLockedBody: This text is no longer editable until you use "Edit source" in the top toolbar. Unlocking it will discard the translation, stage outputs, and audit for this chunk.
document.emptyBody: Prepare a text into multiple chunks to use the document reader, or switch back to Sandbox for quick single-text work.

overview.title: What is Glossa?
overview.p1: Glossa is a multi-stage AI translation pipeline designed for scholars, philologists, and professional translators. Instead of a single-shot translation, it chains multiple LLM passes — each with its own instructions and model — to iteratively refine the output.
overview.p2: An AI judge then audits the final translation against your glossary and quality criteria, assigning a semantic quality label and listing specific issues with suggested fixes.
overview.step1: Paste or import your source text
overview.step3: Stage 2+ refine the translation (fluency, register, etc.)
overview.step4: AI Judge audits: quality label, issues, suggested fixes
overview.step5: You edit the candidate translation manually
overview.p3: Each stage can use a different model and provider. For example, use Gemini for a fast initial draft, then Claude for stylistic refinement, and GPT-4o as the judge. Or run everything locally with Ollama.

pipeline.title: Pipeline Configuration
pipeline.intro: All pipeline settings are accessible from the configuration panel (gear icon in the header). The panel opens as a side drawer with 3 tabs and does not obscure the document. Here's how to set up a translation run:
pipeline.configDesc: Select source and target languages from the dropdowns. Use the swap button (⇄) to quickly reverse the direction. Enable 'Auto-Segment' to split the text into paragraph-level chunks for independent processing.
pipeline.stagesDesc: Each stage represents one LLM pass. Click + to add stages. Expand a stage to configure its provider, model, and prompt. Use the wand icon to have the AI rewrite the prompt, or save/load prompts as reusable templates with the bookmark buttons. You can disable stages without deleting them.
pipeline.runDesc: After pasting your text in the center panel and clicking 'Stage Content', press 'Begin Pipeline'. Each stage runs sequentially per chunk, and you'll see tokens stream in real time. The judge runs automatically after all stages complete.
pipeline.editDesc: The 'Candidate Translation' box is fully editable. Make manual corrections, then click 'Re-Evaluate Drafts' in the audit panel to get an updated quality judgment on your edits.
pipeline.tipDesc: Use different models for different stages. A fast model (Gemini Flash, DeepSeek) works well for initial drafts, while a stronger model (GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet) excels at refinement and judging. Ollama models are ideal for offline work or sensitive content.

features.title: Advanced Features
features.intro: This section covers the most recent additions to Glossa.
features.configDrawerDesc: The configuration panel opens via the gear icon in the header. It is organised into three tabs: Stages (model, provider and prompt for each translation stage), Audit (AI judge configuration and evaluation templates), Registry (glossary term management). The panel is a side drawer that overlays without obstructing the document text.
features.documentToolsDesc: The source and translation editors now use a collapsible toolbar so the chrome disappears when you just want to read or edit raw text. Candidate translations can be validated and locked with the green check button, while the full output of any stage opens in a dedicated modal when you click its status badge in the chunk row.
features.templatesDesc: Every prompt textarea — in each stage and in the Audit Guard — has save and load template buttons. Click the bookmark (+) to save the current prompt under a name; click the open book to browse and load saved templates (with search). Templates are separated by context: stage templates do not appear in the Audit section and vice versa. Saving a template with an existing name updates it automatically.
features.refineDesc: The wand icon next to the Prompt label in each stage and in the Audit Guard sends the current prompt to the AI using the provider and model already selected for that stage. The AI rewrites it to be more professional and effective: a specialised meta-prompt for multi-stage translation pipelines is used for stages; a QA-evaluation meta-prompt is used for the Audit Guard. The result appears immediately in the textarea — edit it freely or press Ctrl+Z to undo.
features.tokenDesc: The header displays in real time the total tokens consumed and the estimated cost for the current session, based on official pricing for each provider. Tokens are tracked separately for each stage and for the judge. The counter updates after each completed chunk.
features.sandboxDesc: The Sandbox button in the header toggles between two working modes. Sandbox is ideal for quick tests, model comparisons, and short single-text translations without chunks. Document mode activates the chunk reader, navigable index, insights panel, and advanced segmentation controls — suited for longer texts translated in multiple independent chunks.
features.exportDesc: Export buttons are located in the Summary section of the header (visible when there are completed chunks). Export as .txt for the translated text only, or as .md for a bilingual Markdown document that includes the original text, translation, audit quality, and any issues flagged per chunk.

streaming.title: Real-time Streaming
streaming.p1: All translation stages stream tokens in real time. As the LLM generates text, you see it appear word by word in the stage output box. This gives immediate feedback and lets you spot issues early without waiting for the full response.
streaming.p2: Streaming works across all five providers: Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, and Ollama. The connection uses Server-Sent Events (SSE) on the backend, with Tauri events relaying tokens to the frontend.
streaming.p3: The AI Judge does not stream — it returns structured JSON (quality rating + issues) which is displayed once complete. If a stage fails during streaming, the system retries automatically with exponential backoff (up to 3 attempts).

audit.title: Audit & Quality Control
audit.intro: The right panel shows audit results from the AI Judge. After the pipeline completes, each chunk receives a semantic quality label. The judge produces four categories of issues:
audit.glossaryIssue: The translation deviates from the terms defined in your Keyword Registry.
audit.accuracyIssue: The meaning of the source text is not faithfully rendered in the translation.
audit.fluencyIssue: The translation reads unnaturally or has awkward phrasing in the target language.
audit.grammarIssue: Grammatical errors, incorrect conjugations, or syntactic issues in the output.
audit.reeval: After manually editing the candidate translation, click 'Re-Evaluate Drafts' to run the judge again on your updated text. This lets you iterate until the quality judgment meets your standards without re-running the entire pipeline.
audit.issuesNav: Issue cards in the audit open the relevant chunk in the document reader. Jumping to the exact text fragment is still heuristic until the judge returns structured offsets.

projects.title: Projects & Files
projects.intro: Glossa saves your work in local SQLite — all data stays on your machine.
projects.createDesc: You can start working immediately in an unsaved draft. When you want to persist it, click Save in the header: on the first save a lightweight dialog asks for the project name. The Projects panel remains useful for opening, explicitly creating, or deleting saved projects.
projects.saveDesc: Click the save icon at any time to persist the current state. When you reopen a project, the full pipeline config, stages, glossary, and translations are restored exactly as you left them.
projects.importExportDesc: Use the upload icon to import a .txt or .md file as source text. Export buttons appear in the Summary section of the header when chunks are completed: export as .txt for the translated text, or as .md for a bilingual Markdown document with original text, translation, audit quality, and issues.

providers.title: LLM Providers
providers.intro: Glossa supports five providers. Each stage and the judge can use a different provider and model. Configure API keys in Settings.
providers.anthropicNote: Claude models. Excellent for nuanced, literary text.
providers.deepseekNote: Chinese AI lab. Competitive pricing, good multilingual support.
providers.ollamaNote: Runs locally. No API key. Fully private.
providers.security: API keys are stored in your operating system's keychain (GNOME Keyring on Linux, Keychain on macOS, Credential Manager on Windows). They are never written to disk in plain text and never sent anywhere except to the provider's API endpoint.

ollama.title: Using Ollama
ollama.intro: Ollama lets you run open-source LLMs entirely on your machine. No API key needed, no data leaves your computer. This is ideal for sensitive documents or offline work.
ollama.installDesc: Download from ollama.com or use the install script:
ollama.pullDesc: Download the model you want to use. Popular choices for translation:
ollama.serveDesc: Ollama must be running in the background. If it's not already:
ollama.useDesc: Open Settings — the Ollama section shows connection status and available models. Select 'ollama' as the provider in any stage or the judge, then pick a model from the dropdown.
ollama.recommendedDesc: For general translation: llama3.2 (8B), mistral (7B), or gemma2 (9B). For higher quality on a capable machine: llama3.1:70b or mixtral:8x7b. Smaller models (< 3B parameters) may produce lower quality translations.

glossary.title: Library: Dictionaries & Prompt Templates
glossary.intro: Glossa includes a global library of reusable dictionaries shared across projects. Each dictionary holds source→translation pairs with optional notes. You can assign a dictionary to a project, edit its entries, and bulk-import terms from CSV files.
glossary.libraryDesc: Click the Library icon in the header (General cluster, far right) to open the panel. In the Dictionaries tab you can create new dictionaries, rename them (double-click), duplicate them (Fork), delete them, and import terms from CSV/TSV. Expand a dictionary to edit its entries inline.
glossary.csvDesc: Click the Upload button next to any dictionary to import terms from a file. A wizard shows a preview of the first 5 rows and asks for a strategy: «Add/update» adds new terms without touching existing ones; «Replace all» deletes all entries and reimports from the file. Recognised headers: term/source/from for the source term, translation/target/to for the translation (case-insensitive). The notes column is optional.
glossary.projectDesc: In the Project Configuration panel (gear icon → Registry tab) you'll find a dropdown to select the dictionary assigned to the project. Edit entries inline in the editor: changes affect the shared dictionary and are persisted with the Save button that appears as soon as you make a change. The same dictionary can be assigned to multiple projects: entries are shared, and updating an entry from one project updates it for all.
glossary.highlightDesc: In the document reader and Sandbox mode, the Highlight terms button (brush icon) in the chunk action bar activates a visual overlay. In the source text, terms present in the dictionary are underlined in blue. In the candidate translation, correctly translated terms appear with a green background; source terms found but with a missing or different translation appear with a red background. Hover over a highlighted term to see the expected translation and any notes.
glossary.auditDesc: The AI Judge specifically checks adherence to the assigned dictionary. If the translation uses a different term than what's defined in the registry, it will flag a 'glossary' issue with severity and a suggested fix. This is especially valuable for technical, legal, or academic texts where terminology must be consistent.
glossary.templatesDesc: In the Library panel (Prompt Templates tab) you manage the global collection of reusable templates. Create a template by specifying a name, context (Stage or Audit), and prompt text. Use the wand button in the creation form to have the AI rewrite the prompt: select the provider and model from the inline dropdowns and click the wand. Saved templates are available in all translation stages and in the Quality Control section via the bookmark buttons (save/load).

shortcuts.title: Interface Reference
shortcuts.lockTranslation: Lock / unlock a validated candidate translation
shortcuts.openStageTrace: Open the full output of a stage
shortcuts.loadTemplate: Open book — load a saved template

library.title: Library
library.dictionariesDesc: Manage reusable term dictionaries across projects.
library.noDictionaries: No dictionaries. Use + to create one.
library.dictionaryDeleteMessage: Delete dictionary "{{name}}"? This action is irreversible.
library.noTemplates: No templates. Use + to create one.
library.csvPickDesc: Supported formats: CSV, TSV. Expected headers: term/source/from and translation/target/to.
library.csvStrategy_replace_desc: removes existing terms and imports new ones
library.csvEmptyError: File has no data or is too short.
